vlm.after_observation: Heavily hazy, low-resolution, pinkish-tinted image. Building outlines are barely visible as blurred color blobs. The red-roofed structure near the reticle is still vaguely present as a reddish patch, but no clear roof geometry, wall edges, or ground-level detail can be resolved. No debris or shadow anomalies can be discerned due to image degradation.
vlm.image_alignment: Apparent registration is acceptable; the reticle is positioned on the same general building footprint and the overall scene orientation matches. However, severe post-event quality loss limits comparison utility.
vlm.image_quality: Before: good sharpness, 1% cloud cover, usable. After: very poor — heavy pinkish haze/smoke, low effective resolution, strong color cast obscuring structural detail. Not adequate for confident triage.
vlm.uncertainty_reason: Post-event imagery is too degraded (smoke/haze, low resolution, color distortion) to confirm or rule out damage at this centroid. The before image is clean, but the after image does not provide sufficient visual evidence to assess rubble, roof loss, or collapse. Manual re-imagery or higher-quality post-event acquisition is recommended before assigning a damage class.
vlm.before_source: Vantor Open Data pre-event reference scene B160001100FD1910, 2026-03-20T14:46:55Z, LG06, 1% cloud cover, not official EMS before imagery

vlm.change_evidence: No change assessment possible. The after image is severely degraded with extreme pixelation/blurring, preventing identification of individual building footprints, roofs, or structural details at the reticle centroid. While the before shows a clearly intact building (white/light roof structure) at the reticle, the after shows only indistinct pinkish/purplish pixel blocks where no structures can be resolved.
vlm.before_observation: Sharp, high-resolution Vantor pre-event imagery. A clearly visible building with a light-colored (white/gray) flat roof sits directly at the reticle centroid, surrounded by densely packed urban structures with red/orange tile roofs, narrow streets, and small yards. No signs of pre-event damage visible.
vlm.after_observation: Heavily pixelated, low-resolution EMS post-event chip. Individual buildings, roof edges, and street geometry are not resolvable. The scene appears as a mosaic of soft pink, mauve, and pale blocks with the reticle visible but no identifiable structure at the centroid. Cannot confirm presence, absence, or condition of the target building.
vlm.image_alignment: Reticle appears in approximately the same screen-relative position in both chips, but the after image's heavy downsampling/pixelation makes true co-registration of fine features unverifiable.
vlm.image_quality: Before: high resolution, sharp, good contrast. After: very low effective resolution, large blocky pixels, no discernible structural detail; quality is insufficient for damage triage at building scale.
vlm.uncertainty_reason: The after (EMS) chip is far too coarse/pixelated to resolve the target building or any debris/rubble signature. A reliable before/after comparison at this scale is not possible from the provided chips, so the official EMS 'Possibly damaged / 35%' label cannot be visually confirmed or refuted here. Recommend re-acquiring a higher-resolution post-event tile over this centroid before assigning a damage class.
vlm.before_source: Vantor Open Data pre-event reference scene B160001100FD1910, 2026-03-20T14:46:55Z, LG06, 1% cloud cover, not official EMS before imagery
